---
title: "Geometry And Coupling"
sidebar: scenarios
---

Geometry and coupling are separate optical-distance tracks because their boundaries rarely align exactly.

## Proposal Sketch

```python
optical_path = inv.OpticalPath(
    name="Garden path",
    optical_components=(
        inv.FiberSegment(name="lead-in", optical_length=50.0),
        inv.FiberSegment(name="sensing fiber", optical_length=720.0),
    ),
    geometries=(
        inv.Geometry(
            optical_length=300.0,
            coordinates=((0.0, 0.0, 0.0), (300.0, 0.0, 0.0)),
        ),
        inv.Geometry(
            optical_length=420.0,
            coordinates=((300.0, 0.0, 0.0), (720.0, 80.0, 0.0)),
        ),
    ),
    coupling_conditions=(
        inv.CouplingCondition(optical_length=470.0, coupling_type="trench"),
        inv.CouplingCondition(optical_length=300.0, coupling_type="surface"),
    ),
)
```

The component, geometry, and coupling tracks can have different interval lengths and boundaries while still describing the same optical path.

## Projection

```python
patch = patch.add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("x", "y", "z", "coupling_type", "label"),
)
```

Projection turns inventory context into patch coordinates only when analysis needs those arrays.
